headline: Timber Royalty Rates Unchanged Since 1985 in Sabah
context: Sabah timber royalty rates remained at RM15-25 per cubic metre since 1985 despite 400% increase in global hardwood prices.

cards.big: Sabah timber royalties frozen at 1985 rates of RM15-25 per cubic metre
cards.sub: Global hardwood prices rose 400% but state revenues remained stagnant

cards.big: Sabah collected only RM280 million in timber royalties in 2025
cards.sub: At market-adjusted rates the state would have earned approximately RM1.1 billion

cards.big: 12 logging concessions covering 800,000 hectares expire between 2026-2030
cards.sub: State government faces pressure from industry to renew at existing low rates

cards.big: Sarawak raised its timber royalties by 60% in 2023 generating RM450 million more
cards.sub: Sabah Forestry Department cited federal interference in rate-setting processes

cards.big: Frozen royalties expose whether state resources benefit the state or the industry
Timber royalties frozen since 1985 — 41 years of unchanged rates while extraction accelerated.
